steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Read circle $M$: $(x-5)^{2}+(y-8)^{2}=9$ is in standard form $(x-h)^{2}+(y-k)^{2}=r^{2}$, so center $= (5,8)$ and $r_{M} = \sqrt{9} = 3$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Circle $P$ has the same center $(5,8)$ and radius $r_{P} = 2 \times r_{M} = 2 \times 3 = 6$, so $r_{P}^{2} = 36$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Circle $P$'s equation is $(x-5)^{2}+(y-8)^{2}=36$. The formula $(x-5)^{2}+(y-8)^{2}=18$ uses $r^{2}=18$, giving $r=3\sqrt{2}\approx4.24$, not twice circle $M$'s radius of 3. The formula $(x-5)^{2}+(y-8)^{2}=36$ has center $(5,8)$ and $r^{2}=36$, so $r=6=2\times3$. The formulas with center $(10,16)$ are eliminated because circle $P$ must keep center $(5,8)$. That leaves B.
